body.body: Some memories belong to a specific Igor instance (episodic, TWM state), others are class-level (CORE_PATTERN, IDENTITY, semantic knowledge). Current model conflates them. Define split: what scopes to instance vs shared. HIGH inertia — models.py touch likely. NOTE: Igor will spec the design (storage split, scope field, table strategy) before this is worker-ready. Do not implement without his spec.
body.notes: | SPEC COMPLETE (D220): scope col (class/instance/session), one table, migrate by memory_type. Prereq: confirm IgorInstance model in models.py.
body.title: Instance vs class memory split
body.result: Added MemoryScope enum (class/instance/session) to models.py. Memory.__post_init__ derives scope from memory_type. DB migration (ALTER TABLE + backfill) runs on next Igor restart for both SQLite and Postgres. get_portable() updated to use scope='class'. 9 tests in test_memory_scope.py. Commit 5d13b39.
